questions.question: Which description applies to a red blood cell? cell wall nucleus A absent absent, B absent present, C present absent, D present present
questions.text: Which description applies to a red blood cell? cell wall nucleus A absent absent, B absent present, C present absent, D present present
options: A absent absent
B absent present
C present absent
D present present

questions.question: A green-leafed plant produces spores. To which group does the plant belong? A dicotyledons, B fungi, C ferns, D monocotyledons
questions.text: A green-leafed plant produces spores. To which group does the plant belong? A dicotyledons, B fungi, C ferns, D monocotyledons
options: A dicotyledons
B fungi
C ferns
D monocotyledons

questions.question: Which statement is always correct for both diffusion and osmosis? A They depend on energy from cells., B They involve the movement of particles down a concentration gradient., C They involve the movement of water molecules., D They take place only in liquids. 3
questions.text: Which statement is always correct for both diffusion and osmosis? A They depend on energy from cells., B They involve the movement of particles down a concentration gradient., C They involve the movement of water molecules., D They take place only in liquids. 3
options: A They depend on energy from cells.
B They involve the movement of particles down a concentration gradient.
C They involve the movement of water molecules.
D They take place only in liquids. 3

questions.question: The graph shows the effect of oxygen on the uptake of ions by two sets of barley plants. uptake of ions by barley plants time high concentration of oxygen available for barley plants low concentration of oxygen available for barley plants Except for oxygen availability, both sets of barley plants were grown in identical conditions. Which process is taking place to explain the difference between the two curves? A active transport, B diffusion, C osmosis, D photosynthesis
questions.text: The graph shows the effect of oxygen on the uptake of ions by two sets of barley plants. uptake of ions by barley plants time high concentration of oxygen available for barley plants low concentration of oxygen available for barley plants Except for oxygen availability, both sets of barley plants were grown in identical conditions. Which process is taking place to explain the difference between the two curves? A active transport, B diffusion, C osmosis, D photosynthesis
options: A active transport
B diffusion
C osmosis
D photosynthesis

questions.question: The  graph  shows  the  effect  of  substrate  concentration  on  the  rate  of  an  enzyme-controlled reaction. The results have been obtained using a fixed concentration of enzyme at a temperature of 30 C. rate of reaction substrate concentration X Y Which statement explains the shape of the graph from X to Y? A Denaturation of the enzyme molecules is increasing., B The enzyme active sites are not all occupied., C The concentration of substrate molecules is decreasing., D The frequency of effective collisions between enzyme active sites and substrate molecules is decreasing.
questions.text: The  graph  shows  the  effect  of  substrate  concentration  on  the  rate  of  an  enzyme-controlled reaction. A Denaturation of the enzyme molecules is increasing., B The enzyme active sites are not all occupied., C The concentration of substrate molecules is decreasing., D The frequency of effective collisions between enzyme active sites and substrate molecules is decreasing.
options: A Denaturation of the enzyme molecules is increasing.
B The enzyme active sites are not all occupied.
C The concentration of substrate molecules is decreasing.
D The frequency of effective collisions between enzyme active sites and substrate molecules is decreasing.

questions.question: The graph shows how the rate of photosynthesis in a plant varies with light intensity. rate of photosynthesis light intensity P QR Which statements are correct? 1     Light intensity is the only limiting factor in photosynthesis. 2     Between points P and Q on the graph, light intensity is the factor that is limiting the rate of photosynthesis. 3     Between Q and R, another factor is limiting the rate of photosynthesis. A 1 and 2 B 1 only C    2 and 3 D 3 only 5
questions.text: The graph shows how the rate of photosynthesis in a plant varies with light intensity. rate of photosynthesis light intensity P QR Which statements are correct? 1     Light intensity is the only limiting factor in photosynthesis. 2     Between points P and Q on the graph, light intensity is the factor that is limiting the rate of photosynthesis. 3     Between Q and R, another factor is limiting the rate of photosynthesis. A 1 and 2 B 1 only C    2 and 3 D 3 only 5
options: A 1 and 2 B 1 only C    2 and 3 D 3 only 5

questions.question: Which description of the function of root hairs is correct? A Root hairs absorb ions by active transport and osmosis., B Root hairs absorb ions by osmosis only., C Root hairs absorb water by active transport., D Root hairs absorb water by osmosis.
questions.text: Which description of the function of root hairs is correct? A Root hairs absorb ions by active transport and osmosis., B Root hairs absorb ions by osmosis only., C Root hairs absorb water by active transport., D Root hairs absorb water by osmosis.
options: A Root hairs absorb ions by active transport and osmosis.
B Root hairs absorb ions by osmosis only.
C Root hairs absorb water by active transport.
D Root hairs absorb water by osmosis.

questions.question: An investigation is carried out on digestion and absorption in the alimentary canal. The diagram shows the apparatus used. The Visking tubing is permeable to small molecules such as glucose but not to large molecules such as starch. beaker water at 36 °C starch solution and amylase Visking tubing glass rod to support Visking tubing After  one  hour,  samples  of water in the beaker  are  tested  with  Benedict’s  solution  and  with iodine solution. Which colours are obtained? colour obtained after heating with Benedict’s solution colour obtained after adding iodine solution A blue blue-black, B blue yellow-brown, C red blue-black, D red yellow-brown 7

questions.question: Some students were each given the apparatus shown. limewater test-tube Ttest-tube U glass tubing rubber bung mouthpiece They  placed  the  mouthpieces  in  their  mouths  and  then  gently  breathed  in  and  out  through  the apparatus ten times. Which statement describes what happened when they breathed in and out? A When  they  breathed  in,  air  from  the  room  bubbled  through  test-tube  U  and  the  limewater became cloudy in test-tube U., B When they breathed out, air from their lungs bubbled through test-tube T and the limewater became cloudy in test-tube T., C When  they  breathed  in,  air  from  the  room  bubbled  through  test-tube  T  and  the  limewater remained clear in test-tube T., D When they breathed out, air from their lungs bubbled through test-tube U and the limewater remained clear in test-tube U.
questions.text: Some students were each given the apparatus shown. limewater test-tube Ttest-tube U glass tubing rubber bung mouthpiece They  placed  the  mouthpieces  in  their  mouths  and  then  gently  breathed  in  and  out  through  the apparatus ten times. Which statement describes what happened when they breathed in and out? A When  they  breathed  in,  air  from  the  room  bubbled  through  test-tube  U  and  the  limewater became cloudy in test-tube U., B When they breathed out, air from their lungs bubbled through test-tube T and the limewater became cloudy in test-tube T., C When  they  breathed  in,  air  from  the  room  bubbled  through  test-tube  T  and  the  limewater remained clear in test-tube T., D When they breathed out, air from their lungs bubbled through test-tube U and the limewater remained clear in test-tube U.
options: A When  they  breathed  in,  air  from  the  room  bubbled  through  test-tube  U  and  the  limewater became cloudy in test-tube U.
B When they breathed out, air from their lungs bubbled through test-tube T and the limewater became cloudy in test-tube T.
C When  they  breathed  in,  air  from  the  room  bubbled  through  test-tube  T  and  the  limewater remained clear in test-tube T.
D When they breathed out, air from their lungs bubbled through test-tube U and the limewater remained clear in test-tube U.

questions.question: Which equation shows the type of respiration that can cause an ‘oxygen debt’ in muscles during vigorous exercise? A glucose + oxygen  carbon dioxide + water, B glucose  lactic acid, C glucose  ethanol + carbon dioxide, D glucose  lactic acid + carbon dioxide + water 8

questions.question: or more 251 What can be concluded from the data? A People who get lung cancer are likely to be smokers of 26 or more cigarettes per day., B People who do not smoke will not get lung cancer., C People who smoke have a higher chance of getting lung cancer., D Smoking is the only cause of lung cancer. 21   What is one way that development of antibiotic-resistant bacteria can be minimised?, A sharing antibiotics with family members, B use of antibiotics only when essential, C use of antibiotics to prevent infection, D use of antibiotics to treat all infections 22   What are the effects of the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) on the immune system? immune system antibody production number of lymphocytes, A becomes weaker decreases decreases, B becomes weaker increases increases, C becomes stronger decreases decreases, D becomes stronger increases increases 10 23   The blood in the renal vein has a different composition to the blood in the renal artery. How is the blood in the renal vein different?, A less carbon dioxide, less urea, less water, B less oxygen, more urea, less water, C more carbon dioxide, less urea, less water, D more oxygen, less urea, less water 24   The diagram shows a reflex arc. X muscle, A person was involved in an accident and their arm was injured. The sensory neurones at X were cut. Which statement describes what happens if this person touches a hot object with their finger?, A The  person  is  not  able  to  feel  their  finger  touching  the  hot  object  but  is  able  to  move  their finger away from the heat stimulus., B The  person  is  not  able  to  feel  their  finger  touching  the  hot  object  or  move  their  finger  away from the heat stimulus., C The person can feel the heat stimulus but is not able to move their finger away from the heat stimulus., D The receptor cells in the person’s finger are not stimulated by the heat. 11 25   The diameter of a person’s pupil is measured as the light intensity is varied. During which time period does the light intensity increase fastest? 10 8 6 4 2 0 diameter of pupil / mm time ABCD 26   On a warm sunny day, a swimmer climbs out of a pool and sits on the edge to dry. After a few minutes, the swimmer’s skin begins to feel cold. What has caused this to happen?, A Body temperature drops until it reaches air temperature., B Muscles and other organs stop producing heat from respiration., C Sunlight causes the hypothalamus to begin processes that increase heat loss., D Water on the skin absorbs heat from the body and evaporates.

During which time period does the light intensity increase fastest? 10 8 6 4 2 0 diameter of pupil / mm time ABCD 26   On a warm sunny day, a swimmer climbs out of a pool and sits on the edge to dry. After a few minutes, the swimmer’s skin begins to feel cold. What has caused this to happen?, A Body temperature drops until it reaches air temperature., B Muscles and other organs stop producing heat from respiration., C Sunlight causes the hypothalamus to begin processes that increase heat loss., D Water on the skin absorbs heat from the body and evaporates.
options: A People who get lung cancer are likely to be smokers of 26 or more cigarettes per day.
B People who do not smoke will not get lung cancer.
C People who smoke have a higher chance of getting lung cancer.
D Smoking is the only cause of lung cancer. 21   What is one way that development of antibiotic-resistant bacteria can be minimised?
A sharing antibiotics with family members
B use of antibiotics only when essential
C use of antibiotics to prevent infection
D use of antibiotics to treat all infections 22   What are the effects of the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) on the immune system? immune system antibody production number of lymphocytes
A becomes weaker decreases decreases
B becomes weaker increases increases
C becomes stronger decreases decreases
D becomes stronger increases increases 10 23   The blood in the renal vein has a different composition to the blood in the renal artery. How is the blood in the renal vein different?
A less carbon dioxide, less urea, less water
B less oxygen, more urea, less water
C more carbon dioxide, less urea, less water
D more oxygen, less urea, less water 24   The diagram shows a reflex arc. X muscle
A person was involved in an accident and their arm was injured. The sensory neurones at X were cut. Which statement describes what happens if this person touches a hot object with their finger?
A The  person  is  not  able  to  feel  their  finger  touching  the  hot  object  but  is  able  to  move  their finger away from the heat stimulus.
B The  person  is  not  able  to  feel  their  finger  touching  the  hot  object  or  move  their  finger  away from the heat stimulus.
C The person can feel the heat stimulus but is not able to move their finger away from the heat stimulus.

D The receptor cells in the person’s finger are not stimulated by the heat. 11 25   The diameter of a person’s pupil is measured as the light intensity is varied. During which time period does the light intensity increase fastest? 10 8 6 4 2 0 diameter of pupil / mm time ABCD 26   On a warm sunny day, a swimmer climbs out of a pool and sits on the edge to dry. After a few minutes, the swimmer’s skin begins to feel cold. What has caused this to happen?
A Body temperature drops until it reaches air temperature.
B Muscles and other organs stop producing heat from respiration.
C Sunlight causes the hypothalamus to begin processes that increase heat loss.
D Water on the skin absorbs heat from the body and evaporates.

questions.question: A genetic disorder causes too much insulin to be secreted. What is the effect of the secretion of too much insulin? A Blood glucose concentration is low., B Glucose can be detected in the urine., C Glycogen level in the liver is low., D Less glucose is converted to glycogen.
questions.text: A genetic disorder causes too much insulin to be secreted. What is the effect of the secretion of too much insulin? A Blood glucose concentration is low., B Glucose can be detected in the urine., C Glycogen level in the liver is low., D Less glucose is converted to glycogen.
options: A Blood glucose concentration is low.
B Glucose can be detected in the urine.
C Glycogen level in the liver is low.
D Less glucose is converted to glycogen.

questions.question: Auxin is a chemical produced by plants. What is the role of auxin? A It stimulates cells in the plant shoot to elongate., B It stimulates meiotic cell division., C It stimulates the development of a branching root system., D It stimulates the process of seed germination. 12
questions.text: Auxin is a chemical produced by plants. What is the role of auxin? A It stimulates cells in the plant shoot to elongate., B It stimulates meiotic cell division., C It stimulates the development of a branching root system., D It stimulates the process of seed germination. 12
options: A It stimulates cells in the plant shoot to elongate.
B It stimulates meiotic cell division.
C It stimulates the development of a branching root system.
D It stimulates the process of seed germination. 12

questions.question: What is a feature of asexual reproduction in a plant? A All the offspring will grow to an identical height., B Any seeds produced will be identical., C The plant needs to carry out the process of meiosis., D The offspring will all have identical genotypes.
questions.text: What is a feature of asexual reproduction in a plant? A All the offspring will grow to an identical height., B Any seeds produced will be identical., C The plant needs to carry out the process of meiosis., D The offspring will all have identical genotypes.
options: A All the offspring will grow to an identical height.
B Any seeds produced will be identical.
C The plant needs to carry out the process of meiosis.
D The offspring will all have identical genotypes.

questions.question: Some  lizards  in  a  population  had  longer  leg  bones  than  others.  They  were  able  to  climb  better than the lizards with shorter legs, so they were able to escape from their predators on the ground. Over  time,  lizards  with  shorter  legs  died  out  so  that  all  the  lizards  in  the  population  now  have longer legs. Which process has caused this change within the lizard population? A extinction, B natural selection, C conservation, D artificial selection
questions.text: Some  lizards  in  a  population  had  longer  leg  bones  than  others.  They  were  able  to  climb  better than the lizards with shorter legs, so they were able to escape from their predators on the ground. Over  time,  lizards  with  shorter  legs  died  out  so  that  all  the  lizards  in  the  population  now  have longer legs. Which process has caused this change within the lizard population? A extinction, B natural selection, C conservation, D artificial selection
options: A extinction
B natural selection
C conservation
D artificial selection

questions.question: The diagram shows a pyramid of biomass. sparrowhawk robin caterpillar lettuce 12 g / m 2 120 g / m 2 600 g / m 2 1200 g / m 2 Which percentage of biomass is passed from the primary consumer to the secondary consumer? A 1% B 10% C 20% D    50% 16

questions.question: The diagram shows part of the carbon cycle. 41 respiration respiration respiration 32 feeding photosynthesis decomposition Which row shows the correct labels for the numbered boxes? 1 2 3 4 A plants the air decomposers animals, B the air plants decomposers animals, C plants the air animals decomposers, D the air plants animals decomposers
questions.text: The diagram shows part of the carbon cycle. 41 respiration respiration respiration 32 feeding photosynthesis decomposition Which row shows the correct labels for the numbered boxes? 1 2 3 4 A plants the air decomposers animals, B the air plants decomposers animals, C plants the air animals decomposers, D the air plants animals decomposers
options: A plants the air decomposers animals
B the air plants decomposers animals
C plants the air animals decomposers
D the air plants animals decomposers

questions.question: Which human activity is likely to contribute most to global warming? A non-biodegradable plastics in the ocean, B pollution due to insecticides, C emissions from burning fossil fuels, D water pollution by sewage
questions.text: Which human activity is likely to contribute most to global warming? A non-biodegradable plastics in the ocean, B pollution due to insecticides, C emissions from burning fossil fuels, D water pollution by sewage
options: A non-biodegradable plastics in the ocean
B pollution due to insecticides
C emissions from burning fossil fuels
D water pollution by sewage

questions.question: Drainage  from  fields  where  nitrogen-containing  fertilisers  have  been  spread  can  result  in  fish dying in rivers. Which sequence of events describes what happens when fish die as a result of water pollution by nitrogen-containing fertilisers? A less   oxygen   dissolved   in   the   water    more   growth   of   water   plants    more   plant decomposition by bacteria, B less  oxygen  dissolved  in  the  water   more  plant  decomposition  by  bacteria   less  plant growth, C more  growth  of  water  plants   less  plant  decomposition  by  bacteria   more  oxygen dissolved in the water, D more  growth  of  water  plants   more  plant  decomposition  by  bacteria   less  oxygen dissolved in the water
questions.text: Drainage  from  fields  where  nitrogen-containing  fertilisers  have  been  spread  can  result  in  fish dying in rivers. Which sequence of events describes what happens when fish die as a result of water pollution by nitrogen-containing fertilisers? A less   oxygen   dissolved   in   the   water    more   growth   of   water   plants    more   plant decomposition by bacteria, B less  oxygen  dissolved  in  the  water   more  plant  decomposition  by  bacteria   less  plant growth, C more  growth  of  water  plants   less  plant  decomposition  by  bacteria   more  oxygen dissolved in the water, D more  growth  of  water  plants   more  plant  decomposition  by  bacteria   less  oxygen dissolved in the water
options: A less   oxygen   dissolved   in   the   water    more   growth   of   water   plants    more   plant decomposition by bacteria
B less  oxygen  dissolved  in  the  water   more  plant  decomposition  by  bacteria   less  plant growth
C more  growth  of  water  plants   less  plant  decomposition  by  bacteria   more  oxygen dissolved in the water
D more  growth  of  water  plants   more  plant  decomposition  by  bacteria   less  oxygen dissolved in the water
